summary: The original Nextra docs scaffold bead was a monolithic parent that explicitly called for decomposition. The decomposition was completed and recorded in .flywheel/audit/flywheel-38u3d/evidence.md; phase work now lives in child/sister beads. The parent cannot be closed while br sees an open child, so it is reclassified to blocked to prevent bv/br from routing workers to the stale container while preserving the actual remaining rollout work in the child graph.
not_a_claim: This does not claim all client docs sites are complete.
This does not claim Phase 3 or Phase 4 rollout is unblocked.
This does not claim the upstream Jeffrey Premium scaffold template issue is fixed.

decomposition_evidence.summary: Declined + decomposed evidence pack with four phase beads.
Compliance pack scored the decomposition disposition at 940/1000.

phase_state.scope: flywheel docs init subcommand and project-type detection
dogfood docs site on Flywheel repo with personas, Diataxis IA, and doctrine pages

phase_state.blocker: deferred until upstream scaffold compatibility and retry trigger are green

phase_state.scope: blackfoot, terratitle, and vrtx docs rollout
phase_state.blocker: blocked by deferred Phase 3 / client onboarding readiness

validation.summary: br refused parent close because flywheel-38u3d still has an open child; reclassification avoids force-closing over the child graph.

value_added: Removes a stale monolithic parent from ready triage.
Keeps remaining docs rollout work visible in child beads instead of hiding it behind the parent.
Preserves the documented Jeffrey-substrate boundary: consume/read the docs-site skill, do not mutate it.
